description: Coca-Cola launched the 'Share a Coke' campaign in Australia in 2011, replacing its logo with the 150 most popular names. The initiative successfully drove massive user-generated content across social media as consumers shared photos of their personalized bottles, resulting in a 7% increase in young adult consumption.

key_events.description: Coca-Cola Australia announces the campaign with localized TV spots.
Viral surge as Twitter users share photos of bottles with specific names.
Retailer feedback confirms stock shortages due to high demand for specific names.
Competitors criticize the campaign for promoting unhealthy sugary habits.

calibration_notes: Excellent for measuring the viral coefficient and the shift from 'undecided' to 'pro' as social proof accumulates.
